assertions.text: V1 was traveling eastbound on a two-lane, non-divided, bidirectional roadway.
The roadway had a slight curve to the left along the segment where V1 was traveling.
The roadway had a positive grade (uphill) where V1 was traveling.
V1 departed the roadway to the right side of travel.
V1's frontal plane impacted a large tree (impact to the front of V1).
After the collision, V1 came to final rest in the eastbound lane of travel.
At final rest V1 was facing south.
There was one impact in which V1's front struck a large tree.

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry provides speed samples ([98.0 km/h at t = -5.0 s and 92.0 km/h at t = 0.0 s](T2)) and vehicle identity ([V1](T1)), but contains no heading, lane count, roadway type, or map data to confirm eastbound direction or two-lane non-divided roadway.

The sensor set includes speed and crash-pulse delta-V channels ([speed samples](T2), [delta-V summaries](T1)(T2)) but no lane geometry, GPS track, or yaw/heading series tied to road curvature are present in the provided telemetry, so curvature (left vs right) is unverifiable.
The available telemetry contains speed and crash-pulse data ([speed](T2), [delta-V](T2)) but no elevation, inclinometer, or grade measurement; therefore roadway grade is unverifiable.

While the crash pulse shows a large frontal deceleration ([final longitudinal delta-V = -92.0 km/h](T2)), there is no GPS lateral position or mapped lane data to establish which side of the roadway the vehicle left, so departure-to-the-right cannot be determined from the telemetry.

The EDR crash-pulse is overwhelmingly longitudinal with a large negative longitudinal delta-V of [‑95.0 km/h peak](T1) and a final longitudinal delta-V of [‑92.0 km/h](T2), indicating a strong deceleration consistent with a frontal impact; additionally the record lists a single event for this vehicle ([num_events_recorded = 1](T1)).

The telemetry shows post-impact delta-V values and no subsequent movement beyond the crash pulse ([final longitudinal delta-V = -92.0 km/h](T2)), but contains no GPS position, lane identification, or scene map to confirm that final rest was specifically in the eastbound lane; thus this location detail is unverifiable.

There are no heading or yaw-angle channels in the provided telemetry to determine final vehicle orientation (the EDR lists crash-pulse timing and delta-V magnitudes [T1,T2] but not final heading), so facing-south is unverifiable.

The EDR indicates [num_events_recorded = 1](T1) and the crash pulse shows a dominant longitudinal delta-V ([max = -95.0 km/h](T1), [final = -92.0 km/h](T2)), supporting that there was one major impact to the front of V1. The object being a "large tree" is not recorded in the telemetry.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a single, very large frontal crash pulse ([peak longitudinal delta-V = -95.0 km/h at 125.0 ms](T1) with [final longitudinal delta-V = -92.0 km/h](T2)) and steady pre-crash speeds ([98.0 km/h → 92.0 km/h](T2)), supporting a severe frontal impact; road geometry, final position, heading and object type (tree) are not in the sensors and thus are unverifiable.

citations.claim: 98.0 km/h at t = -5.0 s and 92.0 km/h at t = 0.0 s

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
